207,simple,MK-TER80-26,,Minnkota Terrova 80,1,0,visible,A GPS-enabled trolling motor with spot-lock technology. Set your position and fish hands-free.,"

The Minnkota Terrova 80 is the trolling motor serious anglers choose. With 80 lbs of thrust and built-in i-Pilot GPS, you get Spot-Lock anchor technology that holds your boat within 5 feet of a waypoint, even in wind and current.

\n

The brushless, variable-speed motor runs quieter and more efficiently than conventional trolling motors, and the integrated Bluetooth lets you control everything from your smartphone. AutoPilot maintains a heading so you can focus on fishing, not steering.

\n

Vertical jigging for walleye, working a weed edge for bass, the Terrova 80 makes you a more effective angler. Stop in to see it mounted on our demo boat.

",,,taxable,,backorder,,,0,0,,,,,0,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,0,Minn Kota,MK-TER80-26,2026,new,on_order,"Thrust

column2: Brushless",1,Once you fish with Spot-Lock you'll never go back. The Terrova 80 is the most-requested trolling motor upgrade we do. Pairs perfectly with any of our Princecraft fishing boats.,,,,,,,,,,,,

column1: 1034,simple,,,2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 21-2S Fishing Pontoon,1,0,visible,"21-foot fishing pontoon with dedicated casting platforms, livewell, and rod storage. The pontoon for anglers who want stability and deck space without giving up fishing-specific features.","The 2023 Princecraft Sportfisher 21 2S is the fishing pontoon for anglers who don't want to choose between a stable platform and serious fishing features. The Sportfisher line is Princecraft's dedicated fishing pontoon, distinct from their general-purpose pontoon lineup. Where typical pontoons are built for cruising and entertaining, the Sportfisher is laid out for casting, trolling, and landing fish.

\nThe hull is twin aluminum pontoon tubes built to Princecraft's pontoon-construction standard, engineered for the chop and wakes of bigger Northern Ontario lakes. The 21-foot length gives you the deck space for multiple anglers without the docking and trailering complexity of the longer 23 and 25-foot variants.

\nFishing-specific layout is what makes the Sportfisher worth the price difference over a base pontoon. Dedicated front and rear casting decks give two anglers room to work in any wind direction. The integrated livewell is sized for a serious day's catch, not the afterthought tank you find on cruising pontoons. Rod storage is built-in, not aftermarket-mounted. Trolling-friendly seat positioning lets you fish from anywhere on the boat without rearranging furniture.

\nThe pontoon platform is what shifts the calculation versus a deep-V fishing boat. Stability for kids, older anglers, or anyone who's done with rolling around in a 17-footer. Deck space for shore lunches, cooler stowage, and equipment that doesn't fit on a traditional fishing hull. And it still fishes hard, because Princecraft built the Sportfisher line specifically for that use case.
\nThe 2S designation indicates the specific trim package; check with Reyco for exact engine, electronics, and accessory configuration included.

\nAvailable at Reyco Marine and Small Engine Ltd. in Sault Ste. Marie. Financing through Reyco's standard partners; rigging, motor installation, and electronics setup handled in-house.",,,taxable,,1,,,0,0,,,,,1,,,,Uncategorized,,,"http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/AttachmentImage-2.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/AttachmentImage.jpg, http://reyco.glvmarketing.ca/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/AttachmentImage-1.jpg",,,,,,,,,0,Princecraft,34493,2023,new,in_stock,"Make

column2: B",2,"The Sportfisher pontoon is what I sell to anglers who used to fish hard from a deep-V and are ready for something more comfortable without giving up the fishing. The 21-footer is the size that works for most Northern Ontario applications: big enough for Lake Superior bays in fair weather, manageable for trailering, and stable enough that grandkids and older relatives can come along. What separates the Sportfisher from a regular pontoon is the layout. Cruising pontoons have the chairs in the wrong places for fishing. The Sportfisher has casting platforms where you actually need them, livewell where it should be, rod storage that isn't an afterthought. It fishes like a fishing boat but you've got the deck space of a pontoon. For families who want one boat that fishes serious and entertains comfortably, this is the answer in the 21-foot range. Match it with a trolling motor, a fishfinder, and the right outboard, and you've got a setup that handles 80 percent of what most anglers in this market actually do.
